links.title: Layer Collapse in Diffusion Language Models

primary_link.title: Layer Collapse in Diffusion Language Models

papers.summary: Diffusion language models (DLMs) have recently emerged as competitive alternatives to autoregressive (AR) language models, yet differences in their activation dynamics remain poorly understood. We characterize these dynamics in LLaDA-8B and identify a striking layer-collapse property: a few early layers exhibit highly similar, collapsed activation patterns dominated by a single large super-outlier persisting over a long token range. Despite its apparent redundancy, this outlier is critical: prun

primary_link.title: Towards Annotation-Free Validation of MLLMs: A Vision-Language Logical Consistency Metric

papers.summary: Dominant accuracy evaluation might reward unwarranted guessing of Large Language Models, and it might not be applicable to novel tasks for model validation without ground-truth (gt) annotation. Based on basic logic principle, we propose a novel framework to evaluate the vision-language logical consistency of MLLMs on both sufficient and necessary cause-effect relations. We define Vision-Language Logical Consistency Metric (VL-LCM) on traditional MC-VQA tests, and recent NaturalBench tests withou
papers.summary_zh: 主流的 accuracy evaluation 可能會獎勵大型語言模型 (Large Language Models) 不必要的猜測，並且在缺乏 ground-truth (gt) 標註的情況下，它可能不適用於新任務的模型驗證 (model validation)。基於基本邏輯原理，我們提出了一個新穎的框架，用於評估 MLLMs 在充分和必要因果關係 (cause-effect relations) 上的 vision-language logical consistency。我們在傳統的 MC-VQA tests 以及近期沒有 g的 NaturalBench tests 上定義了 Vision-Language Logical Consistency Metric (VL-LCM)。

papers.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents now execute long, tool-using tasks where final outcome checks can arrive too late for intervention. Online warning requires lightweight prefix monitors over heterogeneous traces, but hand-authored event schemas are brittle and deployment-time LLM judging is costly. We argue that the most informative evaluation signal lies at the boundary, where the per-prompt pass probability is near $0.5$ under random-sampling decoding, and propose Dynamic Boundary Evaluation (DBE), which actively locates each model's boundary and places it on a globally comparable difficulty scale. primary_link.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity

papers.summary: Large language models are typically deployed as monolithic systems, requiring the full model even when applications need only a narrow subset of capabilities, e.g., code, math, or domain-specific knowledge. Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) seemingly offer a potential alternative by activating only a subset of experts per input, but in practice, restricting inference to a subset of experts for a given domain leads to severe performance degradation. papers.summary: We introduce Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a reinforcement learning approach for training recursive agents: agents that can spawn and delegate sub-tasks to new instantiations of themselves recursively. Recursive agents implement an inference-time scaling algorithm that naturally allows agents to scale to longer contexts and generalize to more difficult problems via divide-and-conquer. RAO provides a method to train models to best take advantage of such recursive inference, teaching agents

links.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems

primary_link.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems

papers.summary: Large language model (LLM)-based Multi-agent systems (MAS) have shown promise in tackling complex collaborative tasks, where agents are typically orchestrated via role-specific prompts. While the quality of these prompts is pivotal, jointly optimizing them across interacting agents remains a non-trivial challenge, primarily due to the misalignment between local agent objectives and holistic system goals. To address this, we introduce MASPO, a novel framework designed to automatically and iterati
papers.summary_zh: 基於大型語言模型 (LLM) 的 Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) 在處理複雜協作任務方面展現出潛力，其中代理通常透過 role-specific prompts 進行協調。雖然這些 prompts 的品質至關重要，但在相互作用的代理之間聯合優化它們仍然是一個不小的挑戰，這主要是由於局部代理目標與整體系統目標之間的不一致。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了 MASPO，這是一個旨在自動且迭代地進行設計的新型框架。

links.title: SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents

primary_link.title: SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents

papers.summary: LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed to handle streaming tasks, yet they often remain one-off problem solvers that fail to learn from past interactions. Reusable skills distilled from experience provide a natural substrate for self-evolution, where high-quality skill curation serves as the key bottleneck. Existing approaches either rely on manual skill curation, prescribe heuristic skill operations, or train for short-horizon skill operations. However, they still struggle to learn complex
papers.summary_zh: LLM-based agents 越來越多地被部署來處理串流任務，然而它們往往仍是無法從過往互動中學習的一次性問題解決者。從經驗中提煉出的可重用 skills 為自我演化提供了自然的基礎，其中高品質的 skill curation 是關鍵瓶頸。現有方法要麼依賴手動 skill curation，要麼規定 heuristic skill operations，要麼針對 short-horizon skill operations 進行訓練。然而，它們仍然難以學習複雜的 skills。

papers.summary_zh: 我們介紹了 PACZero，這是一系列 PAC-private zeroth-order 機制，用於 fine-tuning 大型語言模型，並能在 $I(S^*; Y_{1:T})=0$ 時提供可用的 utility。這種隱私機制將 membership-inference attack (MIA) 的後驗成功率限制在先驗水平，這是 DP 框架僅在 $\varepsilon=0$ 和無限噪音下才能達到的 MIA 抵抗水平。下面所有的 DP-ZO 比較都在 MIA 後驗水平上進行匹配。關鍵的見解是，PAC Privacy 僅在互信息不為零時才收取費用。

primary_link.title: Invariant Features in Language Models: Geometric Characterization and Model Attribution

papers.summary: Language models exhibit strong robustness to paraphrasing, suggesting that semantic information may be encoded through stable internal representations, yet the structure and origin of such invariance remain unclear. papers.summary_zh: 語言模型對 paraphrasing 表現出強大的魯棒性，這表明語義資訊可能透過穩定的內部表示進行編碼，然而這種不變性的結構和起源仍不清楚。我們提出了一個局部幾何框架，其中語義等效的輸入在 latent space 中佔據結構化區域，伴隨著沿著 nuisance directions 的 paraphrastic 變化，以及在 invariant subspaces 中保留的語義同一性。基於這一觀點，我們提出了三項貢獻。

primary_link.title: Pop Quiz Attack: Black-box Membership Inference Attacks Against Large Language Models

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) show strong performance across many applications, but their ability to memorize and potentially reveal training data raises serious privacy concerns. We introduce the PopQuiz Attack, a black-box membership inference attack that tests whether a model can recall specific training examples. papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 在許多應用中都表現出強大的性能，但它們記憶並可能洩露訓練數據的能力引發了嚴重的隱私問題。我們介紹了 PopQuiz Attack，這是一種黑盒 membership inference attack，該攻擊測試模型是否能回憶起特定的訓練範例。核心思想是將目標數據轉化為測驗式 multiple-choice questions，並從模型的答案中推斷 membership。我們針對六個廣泛使用的 LLMs (GPT-3.5, GPT-4o, LLaMA2-7b, ...) 進行了評估。

papers.summary: Autonomous LLM agents face a critical security risk known as workflow hijacking, where attackers subtly alter tool and skill invocations. papers.summary_zh: 自主 LLM 代理面臨一個關鍵的安全風險，稱為 workflow hijacking，其中攻擊者會巧妙地更改工具和技能的調用。現有的防禦措施依賴於主機內部的遙測資料（例如 audit logs），如果主機 OS 被入侵，這些資料可能會被偽造。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了 ClawGuard，這是一個被動的、頻外監控器，它利用電磁 (EM) 輻射來審計 LLM 代理的工作流程。因為獨特的代理技能會產生獨特的硬體使用模式（計算、DRAM、網路...）。

papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLM) 代理越來越被期望能維持連貫的、長期的個性化記憶，然而，目前的基準測試主要衡量靜態的事實檢索，忽略了在新證據出現時修正儲存信念的能力。我們識別出一個關鍵且尚未充分探索的失敗模式，即「隱性衝突 (Implicit Conflict)」：後來的觀察在沒有明確否定下使早期記憶失效，需要語境推斷和常識推理來檢測。為了嚴格評估此

papers.summary: Activation steering controls LLM behaviour towards target behaviour by intervening in internal representations, yet it often degrades reasoning and retrieval performance. We argue that a primary cause of this trade-off is attention rerouting: steering vectors alter query-key matching, shifting attention away from contextually important tokens toward less informative ones. To address this, we propose Steering via Key-Orthogonal Projections (SKOP), a steering method that constrains harmful attenti
papers.summary_zh: Activation steering 透過干預內部表示來控制 LLM 的行為以達到目標行為，但它通常會降低推理和檢索性能。我們認為這種權衡的一個主要原因是 attention rerouting：steering vectors 改變了 query-key 匹配，將注意力從語境上重要的 tokens 轉移到信息量較少的 tokens。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 Steering via Key-Orthogonal Projections (SKOP)，這是一種限制有害 attention 的 steering 方法。

papers.summary: Safety benchmarks are routinely treated as evidence about how a language model will behave once deployed, but this inference is fragile if behavior depends on whether a prompt looks like an evaluation. papers.summary: The ability to reliably distinguish human-written text from that generated by large language models is of profound societal importance. The dominant approach to this problem exploits the likelihood hypothesis: that machine-generated text should appear more probable to a detector language model than human-written text. papers.summary_zh: 可靠地區分人類撰寫的文本與大型語言模型生成的文本具有深遠的社會重要性。解決此問題的主導方法利用了似然假設：即機器生成的文本對檢測器語言模型來說應該比人類撰寫的文本更可能。然而，我們證明了區分人類與機器文本的詞元級別訊號在檢測器模型的隱藏空間中是非均勻的，並且天真地平均似然。

links.title: DINORANKCLIP: DINOv3 Distillation and Injection for Vision-Language Pretraining with High-Order Ranking Consistency

primary_link.title: DINORANKCLIP: DINOv3 Distillation and Injection for Vision-Language Pretraining with High-Order Ranking Consistency

papers.summary: Contrastive language-image pretraining (CLIP) suffers from two structural weaknesses: the symmetric InfoNCE loss discards the relative ordering among unmatched in-batch pairs, and global pooling collapses the visual representation into a semantic bottleneck that is poorly sensitive to fine-grained local structure. RANKCLIP partially addresses the first issue with a list-wise Plackett-Luce ranking-consistency loss, but its model is strictly first-order and inherits the second weakness untouched.
papers.summary_zh: 對比式語言-圖像預訓練 (CLIP) 存在兩個結構性弱點：對稱式 InfoNCE loss 丟棄了不匹配批內對之間的相對順序，以及全局池化將視覺表示壓縮成一個語義瓶頸，該瓶頸對細粒度局部結構的敏感度很差。RANKCLIP 通過列表級 Plackett-Luce 排序一致性 loss 部分解決了第一個問題，但其模型嚴格為一階，並繼承了第二個弱點而未加處理。

links.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI

primary_link.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI

radar.summary: In this post, you will learn how to implement reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to introduce verification and transparency into reward signals to improve training performance. This approach works best when outputs can be objectively verified for correctness, such as in mathematical reasoning, code generation, or symbolic manipulation tasks. You will also learn how to layer techniques like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and few-shot examples to further improve results. You’ll use the GSM8K dataset (Grade School Math 8K: a collection of grade school math problems) to improve math problem solving accuracy, but the techniques used here can be adapted to a wide variety of other use cases.
radar.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI

links.title: ROSE: Rollout On Serving GPUs via Cooperative Elasticity for Agentic RL

primary_link.title: ROSE: Rollout On Serving GPUs via Cooperative Elasticity for Agentic RL

radar.summary:
Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a key driver for improving the multi-step reasoning and tool-use capabilities of LLMs. However, its efficiency is bottlenecked by long-tail rollouts with multi-turn environment interactions, making static GPU provisioning a poor fit: overprovisioning wastes GPUs on stragglers, while underprovisioning increases contention and slows training.
  We observe that production serving clusters routinely leave substantial GPU compute and memory headroom.
radar.summary_zh: Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) 已成為提升 LLMs 多步驟推理和工具使用能力的關鍵驅動力。然而，其效率受到具有多輪環境互動的長尾 rollouts 所瓶頸限制，使得靜態的 GPU 資源供應不適用：過度供應會浪費 GPU 資源在落後者 (stragglers) 上，而供應不足則會增加競爭並減慢訓練速度。我們觀察到，生產級的 serving clusters 通常會預留大量的 GPU 運算和記憶體餘裕空間 (headroom)。

radar.summary_zh: 已部署的語言模型和視覺-語言模型必須針對每個輸入決定是直接回答、檢索證據、交由更強大的模型處理，還是放棄回答。與常見的單調性直覺相反，在有限樣本中，更高的單輸入表達能力並非總是有益的：在相同的嚴格交叉驗證下，不同的基準測試偏好不同的控制器類別。這反映了實例層級不確定性信號的有限樣本限制，這些信號在分佈上可能會被耗盡
radar.title: A Regime Theory of Controller Class Selection for LLM Action Decisions
radar.title_zh: LLM 行動決策中控制器類別選擇的機制理論

links.title: Safactory: A Scalable Agent Factory for Trustworthy Autonomous Intelligence

primary_link.title: Safactory: A Scalable Agent Factory for Trustworthy Autonomous Intelligence

radar.summary: As large models evolve from conversational assistants into autonomous agents, challenges increasingly arise from long-horizon decision making, tool use, and real environment interaction. Existing agenticinfrastructure remain fragmented across evaluation, data management, and agent evolution, making it difficult to discover risks systematically and improve models in a continuous closed loop. In this report, we present \textbf{Safactory}, a scalable agent factory for trustworthy autonomous intelli
radar.summary_zh: 隨著大型模型從對話助理演進為自主代理（autonomous agents），在長程決策（long-horizon decision making）、工具使用（tool use）和真實環境互動（real environment interaction）方面，挑戰日益增加。現有的代理基礎設施（agent infrastructure）在評估（evaluation）、數據管理（data management）和代理演進（agent evolution）方面仍然分散，使得系統性地發現風險並在連續的閉環（closed loop）中改進模型變得困難。在本報告中，我們介紹了 Safactory，一個用於值得信賴的自主智慧（autonomous intelligence）的可擴展代理工廠（scalable agent factory）。
radar.title: Safactory: A Scalable Agent Factory for Trustworthy Autonomous Intelligence
radar.title_zh: Safactory: 一個可擴展的代理工廠，用於值得信賴的自主智慧

links.title: One Algorithm, Two Goals: Dual Scoring for Parameter and Data Selection in LLM Fine-Tuning

primary_link.title: One Algorithm, Two Goals: Dual Scoring for Parameter and Data Selection in LLM Fine-Tuning

radar.summary: In Large Language Model (LLM) fine-tuning, parameter and data selection are common strategies for reducing fine-tuning cost, yet they are typically driven by separate scoring mechanisms. When a parameter mask and data subset jointly determine restricted fine-tuning, this separation incurs redundant overhead and makes coordinated selection difficult. We cast parameter and data selection as two bilevel selection problems under a common validation objective and derive a shared local response-surrog
radar.summary_zh: 在大型語言模型（LLM）的 fine-tuning 中，參數（parameter）和數據選擇（data selection）是降低 fine-tuning 成本的常用策略，但它們通常由獨立的評分機制（scoring mechanisms）驅動。當參數遮罩（parameter mask）和數據子集（data subset）共同決定受限 fine-tuning 時，這種分離會產生冗餘開銷（redundant overhead）並使協調選擇（coordinated selection）變得困難。我們將參數和數據選擇視為在共同驗證目標（common validation objective）下的兩個雙層選擇問題（bilevel selection problems），並推導出一個共享的局部響應代理（local response-surrog）。
radar.title: One Algorithm, Two Goals: Dual Scoring for Parameter and Data Selection in LLM Fine-Tuning
radar.title_zh: 一個演算法，兩個目標：用於 LLM Fine-Tuning 中參數和數據選擇的雙重評分

links.title: Graphlets as Building Blocks for Structural Vocabulary in Knowledge Graph Foundation Models

primary_link.title: Graphlets as Building Blocks for Structural Vocabulary in Knowledge Graph Foundation Models

radar.summary: Foundation models excel at language, where sentences become tokens, and vision, where images become pixels, because both reduce to discrete symbols on a shared, fixed grid. Knowledge Graphs share the discreteness, but not the geometry. Their entities and relations are discrete symbols, yet their arrangement is relational and lacks a common, fixed grid. Knowledge Graphs (KGs) share the discreteness, but not the geometry. They form irregular, non-Euclidean topologies whose local neighborhoods diff
radar.summary_zh: 基礎模型（Foundation models）擅長語言領域，其中句子（sentences）成為 token；也擅長視覺領域，其中圖像（images）成為像素（pixels），因為兩者都簡化為共享、固定網格（fixed grid）上的離散符號（discrete symbols）。知識圖譜（Knowledge Graphs）共享離散性（discreteness），但非幾何性（geometry）。它們的實體（entities）和關係（relations）是離散符號，但它們的排列是關係性的（relational），並且缺乏一個共同、固定的網格。知識圖譜（KGs）共享離散性，但非幾何性。它們形成不規則的非歐幾里得拓撲（non-Euclidean topologies），其局部鄰域（local neighborhoods）不同。
radar.title: Graphlets as Building Blocks for Structural Vocabulary in Knowledge Graph Foundation Models
radar.title_zh: Graphlets 作為知識圖譜基礎模型中結構詞彙的構建塊

links.title: CrossCult-KIBench: A Benchmark for Cross-Cultural Knowledge Insertion in MLLMs

primary_link.title: CrossCult-KIBench: A Benchmark for Cross-Cultural Knowledge Insertion in MLLMs

radar.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), trained primarily on English-centric data, frequently generate culturally inappropriate or misaligned responses in cross-cultural settings. To mitigate this, we introduce the task of cross-cultural knowledge insertion, which focuses on adapting models to specific cultural contexts while preserving their original behavior in other cultures. radar.summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）主要基於以英語為中心的數據進行訓練，在跨文化環境中經常產生文化上不適當或不一致的回應。為了緩解這個問題，我們引入了跨文化知識插入（cross-cultural knowledge insertion）的任務，該任務專注於使模型適應特定的文化語境，同時在其他文化中保持其原始行為。為了促進該領域的研究，我們引入了 CrossCult-KIBench，一個用於評估的綜合評估基準（comprehensive evaluation benchmark）。
radar.title: CrossCult-KIBench: A Benchmark for Cross-Cultural Knowledge Insertion in MLLMs
radar.title_zh: CrossCult-KIBench：用於 MLLMs 中跨文化知識插入的基準

links.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML

primary_link.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML

top5.summary: Ranking LLMs via pairwise human feedback underpins current leaderboards for open-ended tasks, such as creative writing and problem-solving. We analyze ~89K comparisons in 116 languages from 52 LLMs from Arena, and show that the best-fit global Bradley-Terry (BT) ranking is misleading. Nearly 2/3 of the decisive votes cancel out, and even the top 50 models according to the global BT ranking are statistically indistinguishable (pairwise win probabilities are at most 0.53 within the top 50 models).
top5.summary_zh: 透過配對的人類回饋對 LLMs 進行排名是目前針對開放式任務（例如創意寫作和問題解決）排行榜的基礎。我們分析了來自 Arena 的 52 個 LLMs 在 116 種語言中的約 89K 次比較，並顯示最佳擬合的全球 Bradley-Terry (BT) 排名具有誤導性。近 2/3 的決定性投票相互抵消，甚至根據全球 BT 排名位列前 50 名的模型在統計上是無法區分的（在排名前 50 名的模型中，配對勝率最高為 0.53）。
top5.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
top5.title_zh: 為何全球 LLM 排行榜具有誤導性：用於異質監督式 ML 的小型組合

links.title: Federation of Experts: Communication Efficient Distributed Inference for Large Language Models

primary_link.title: Federation of Experts: Communication Efficient Distributed Inference for Large Language Models

top5.summary:
Mixture of experts has emerged as the primary mechanism for making Large Language Models (LLMs) computationally efficient. However, in distributed settings, communicating token embeddings between experts is a significant bottleneck.

top5.summary_zh: Mixture of experts 已成為使 Large Language Models (LLMs) 達到計算效率的主要機制。然而，在分散式設定中，專家之間傳輸 token embeddings 是一個顯著的瓶頸。我們提出了新穎的 Federation of Experts (FoE) 架構。FoE 將 Transformer 層的 MoE 區塊重構為多個 MoE clusters。每個 cluster 只負責其中一個 KV heads，並且在這些專家之間應用 expert parallelism。
top5.title: Federation of Experts: Communication Efficient Distributed Inference for Large Language Models
top5.title_zh: 專家聯邦：大型語言模型中通訊高效的分散式推論

links.title: Instrumental Choices: Measuring the Propensity of LLM Agents to Pursue Instrumental Behaviors

primary_link.title: Instrumental Choices: Measuring the Propensity of LLM Agents to Pursue Instrumental Behaviors

top5.summary: AI systems have become increasingly capable of dangerous behaviours in many domains. This raises the question: Do models sometimes choose to violate human instructions in order to perform behaviour that is more useful for certain goals? We introduce a benchmark for measuring model propensity for instrumental convergence (IC) behaviour in terminal-based agents. This is behaviour such as self-preservation that has been hypothesised to play a key role in risks from highly capable AI agents. Our ben
top5.summary_zh: AI 系統在許多領域變得越來越能夠執行危險行為。這引出了一個問題：模型有時是否會選擇違反人類指令，以執行對某些目標更有用的行為？我們引入了一個基準，用於衡量基於終端機的 agents 在 instrumental convergence (IC) 行為方面的模型傾向。這種行為（例如自我保存）被認為在來自高能力 AI agents 的風險中扮演關鍵角色。
top5.title: Instrumental Choices: Measuring the Propensity of LLM Agents to Pursue Instrumental Behaviors
top5.title_zh: 工具性選擇：衡量 LLM Agents 追求工具性行為的傾向
